verdict: SAFETY-PASS + STRONG utility -> eligible for frozen-protocol discussion

red_team_down_scope.integrity: COMMIT-CLEAN: no peeking; tau re-derives byte-exact (hexfloat 3f72ca060cce336e, rank 81/88); calibration null-only; eval loads locked tau (never re-derives); monotonicity holds; invalid accounting conservative; no_retune honored; all sha256 pass.
red_team_down_scope.down_scoped_verdict: development-diagnostic; single-block SAFETY-PASS with a thin, drift-exposed margin and ~17% power against a SOFT covariate -- candidate for frozen-protocol DESIGN DISCUSSION, not a confirmatory result.
red_team_down_scope.soft_covariate: #1 caveat: session_auc~0.52, overlap~0.99 in ALL conditions -> type-I control shown only vs a near-negligible covariate. Raw certifier still false-confirms 72/300 here; router works ONLY because artifact-T stays below tau. Strong-covariate regime (router could fail) UNTESTED.
red_team_down_scope.thin_tau_fragile: NULL_cov 5 vs cap 7 (headroom 2); verified 2x calib->valid drift (allow 3->5) with n=1 block (no drift variance); tau*0.90 -> NULL_cov 11 breach; sibling tau_boundary(0.004049) would FAIL; cohort knife-edge min +3.4e-5 above tau.
red_team_down_scope.utility_genuine_but_low: power IS concept-driven (AUC~0.90; confirm-T tracks subject-consistency +0.86 not covariate +0.16) but 17% recall (83% abstained) on an EASY linear 25deg concept matched to a linear certifier; STRONG only vs 6.7% bar; the two POS endpoints are one DGP sampled twice (not independent).
red_team_down_scope.path_to_confirmatory: multiple independent validation blocks (bound drift) + a strong-covariate null (test failure mode) + second dataset / nonlinear concept.
